people.title: People
people.subtitle: Friendly names for DNA fingerprints and workspace collaborators
people.hero_desc: Save friendly names for DNA fingerprints, local users, external DNA Connect members, and workspace collaborators. These names are private to you and do not rename anyone globally.
people.module_missing: The People UI module did not load. Hard-refresh the browser and try again.
people.no_saved_desc: Add your first fingerprint label. Later, workspace members and external invite flows can offer “Add to People” automatically.
people.future_desc: These labels will later appear in @mentions, file locks, comments, activity timelines, “what changed?” digests, and access maps.
people.notes_placeholder: Private note, e.g. John from motorbike club
people.editor.private_label_desc: This only changes your private label for this fingerprint. It does not rename the real user globally.
people.editor.detached_desc: Private label for fingerprints, local users, and external DNA Connect identities. This does not rename anyone globally.
people.confirm.title: Confirm action
people.remove.title: Remove from People?
people.remove.subtitle: This removes your private label for this person.
people.remove.note: This does not delete the real user, external member, or any files. It only removes the saved People label.
admin.settings.title: Admin Settings
admin.settings.notes.title: Notes
admin.settings.notes.body: Settings are admin-only. They affect logging going forward. Already-written audit lines remain unchanged.
admin.settings.top.title: Admin • Settings
admin.audit.verbosity_note: Meaning: DEBUG logs everything • INFO hides noisy lifecycle • ADMIN is default • SECURITY keeps only security-relevant events. Note: already-written audit lines remain; this only affects what gets appended going forward.
admin.audit.rotation_note: Rotation renames the active pqnas_audit.jsonl into a timestamped archive and starts a fresh active log. Retention rules apply only to rotated archives.
admin.audit.rot.size_mb: When active log exceeds N MB
admin.audit.rotation_policy_note: Automatic rotation is enforced server-side (best-effort) before appending audit lines. Retention rules still apply only to rotated archives, never the active log.
admin.audit.ret.size_mb: Keep up to N MB total (rotated files)
admin.audit.retention_safety: Safety: retention prunes rotated archive files only. The active audit log (pqnas_audit.jsonl) is never deleted by retention.
admin.audit.preview_note: Preview is a dry-run: it lists what would be deleted. “Run prune now” deletes only those candidates, and logs an admin audit event.
admin.snap.title: Snapshots (Btrfs)
admin.snap.note1: If Automatic schedule is unchecked while snapshots remain enabled, snapshots are manual only: users/admins can still trigger “snapshot now”, but the scheduler does not run. If per-volume schedule is enabled, each volume can override the global times/day + jitter. Otherwise the global schedule applies to all volumes.
admin.snap.note2: Uses btrfs subvolume snapshot -r. The destination must be an empty path (server cleans up if it exists). If snapshots are disabled, settings stay visible but inputs are greyed out.
admin.uploads.title: Uploads
admin.uploads.note1: These values control how often the migration worker runs and how aggressively it drains landing storage.
admin.uploads.note2: Upload tiering lets new uploads land in a fast pool first, then migrate to capacity storage. Select an internal pool for landing. USB / removable pools are automatically blocked for safety. The migration worker later moves files to capacity pools according to tiering policy.
admin.uploads.help_body:
Upload limits

Upload tiering
New uploads land in a fast internal pool first and later migrate to capacity storage.

USB / removable pools are automatically blocked for safety.

Worker interval
How often the migration worker wakes up and scans landing storage.

Minimum landing age
A file must remain in landing at least this long before migration is allowed.

Max files per pass
Maximum number of eligible files migrated during one worker scan.

Example:
interval = 60
min age = 60
max/pass = 8

Worker wakes every 60 seconds, migrates files older than 60 seconds, and processes up to 8 files each cycle.
admin.dna.title: DNA Connect alerts
admin.dna.note1: PQ-NAS can send warnings to DNA Connect by invoking dna-connect-cli.
admin.dna.note2: A dedicated PQ-NAS DNA identity must already exist in the configured DNA data directory, and the recipient should already have accepted that identity as a contact.
admin.dna.note3: The test button sends a real message using the current saved settings. This is optional and fully admin-controlled.
admin.theme.title: UI Theme
admin.theme.desc: Theme is saved in admin_settings.json and also cached in your browser so all pages can apply it instantly.
admin.confirm.title: Confirm action
admin.confirm.run_prune_sub: This deletes rotated audit archives according to the saved retention policy.
admin.confirm.rotate_sub: This closes the current audit log and starts a fresh active log.
admin.stats.top_sub: Storage overview, file counts, MIME groups, users, and workspaces
admin.users.top_sub: Profile, role, and storage metadata • saved to users.json
admin.users.avatar_placeholder: avatar (click to choose; image will be resized…)
admin.users.help: Use Enable to allow logins, Disable to require approval again, and Revoke to hard-block that fingerprint. Delete removes the entry entirely (cleanup).
admin.users.load_edit_title: Load this user into the edit form
admin.users.enable_title: Allow this fingerprint to log in again
admin.users.disable_title: Disable login until an admin enables it again
admin.users.revoke_title: Hard-block this fingerprint from logging in
admin.users.allocate_title: Allocate storage and set quota for this user
admin.users.migrate_title: Move user storage to another pool with async copy and verify
admin.users.delete_title: Remove this entry from users.json; the user can reappear if they scan again
admin.users.self_protection: Self-protection: enable / disable / revoke / delete are blocked for your own fingerprint.
admin.users.refuse_self: Refusing to modify your own admin entry (prevents lockout or role change).
admin.users.amount_help: Set a positive quota to allow uploads.
admin.users.warn_below_used: Requested quota is below current used space.
admin.users.warn_over_alloc: Requested quota exceeds remaining allocatable capacity on this pool.
admin.users.migrate_help: Migration copies user data to the destination pool, verifies it, then switches the user’s storage mapping. Source data is kept for safety.
admin.users.delete_confirm_sub: This removes the entry from users.json.
admin.users.delete_confirm_note: This removes the entry entirely as cleanup. If they scan again, they will re-appear as disabled.
admin.users.revoke_confirm_sub: This hard-blocks login for this fingerprint.
admin.users.revoke_confirm_note: Use this when this identity should not be allowed to log in again.
admin.users.avatar_read_failed: Could not read this image. Try PNG, JPEG, or WebP.
admin.users.avatar_too_large: Avatar is still too large after resizing ({size}). Try a smaller image.
admin.users.avatar_uploaded_toast:
Avatar uploaded
{note}Click Upsert to save this avatar URL into the user profile.
admin.users.already_allocated_confirm:
Storage is already allocated for this user.

Change pool/quota anyway?
admin.users.storage_required_migration: Storage must be allocated before migration.
admin.users.destination_diff: Destination pool must differ from current pool.
admin.users.migrate_confirm:
Migrate user storage to pool "{pool}"?

This will create an async job. The worker will copy data, verify it, then switch the user's storage mapping.
admin.users.migration_job_missing: Migration job_id missing from server response
admin.users.migration_timeout_detail: Migration polling timed out. Job is still on server; reopen status later.
admin.users.cleanup_sub: Choose the old pool copy to remove for this user.
admin.users.old_pool_placeholder: old pool id, for example raidtest
admin.users.cleanup_note: Only the old inactive storage copy should be removed. The active pool is protected.
admin.users.old_pool_active_error: Old pool id cannot be the active pool.
admin.users.pool_id_not_path: Use a pool id, not a path.
admin.users.cleanup_prompt:
Cleanup old copy from which pool?

User is currently active on {pool}.
Enter old pool id to delete, or use "default" if the stale copy is there.
admin.users.old_pool_must_differ: Old pool must differ from the active pool.
admin.users.cleanup_job_missing: Cleanup job_id missing from server response
admin.users.cleanup_not_needed_toast:
Old storage cleanup not needed
No old inactive copy was found.
admin.approvals.top_sub: Enable disabled users after they scan • saved to users.json
admin.approvals.title: Approvals
admin.approvals.help: This view focuses on identities needing action (disabled / revoked). Use Enable to approve login. Delete removes the entry entirely (cleanup).
admin.approvals.delete_sub: This removes the user from users.json.
admin.approvals.delete_note: This removes the entry entirely as cleanup. If they scan again, they will re-appear as disabled.
admin.approvals.revoke_sub: This hard-blocks login for this fingerprint.
admin.approvals.revoke_note: Use this when the identity should not be allowed to log in again.
admin.apps.sidebar_note: Upload app zip → validate → install. Manage installed apps.
admin.apps.top_sub: Upload app zip → validate → install. Manage installed apps.
admin.apps.install_help: Zip must contain manifest.json and www/index.html.
admin.apps.admin_only_install: Admin only — hide from normal users
admin.apps.uninstall_sub: This removes the installed app package from this server.
admin.apps.uninstall_note: This removes the installed app files and registration for this version. User data stored elsewhere is not intentionally deleted.
admin.audit_ui.sidebar_note: This page only renders and verifies. It never modifies audit log contents.
admin.audit_ui.top_sub: Hash-chained JSONL security audit trail (tail + integrity verification)
admin.audit_ui.tip: Tip: click a row to expand full JSON. Use Esc to collapse all.
admin.audit_ui.interval_title: Auto-refresh interval in seconds (used when Auto is enabled)
admin.audit_ui.footer_note: Expand rows for raw JSON (monospace). This page does not modify the audit log and does not attempt to “fix” anything — it only renders and verifies.
admin.audit_ui.rotate_sub: This creates a rotated archive and starts a fresh active audit log.
admin.audit_ui.rotate_action: Create rotated file and write rotate_header
admin.audit_ui.rotate_note: The page refreshes the tail and verifies integrity after rotation.
admin.overview.sidebar_hint: Manage users, review audit logs, and verify server integrity.

system.selftest_unavailable: Self-test start not available for this drive.

login.hint: Open DNA Connect → QR Reader → scan the QR code.
login.incomplete_session: Server returned no k/st/sid (v5 session incomplete).
login.cookie_failed_http: Consume OK, but cookie did not stick (auth check failed): HTTP {status}
login.not_approved_yet: Not approved yet — waiting for admin approval.

wait.top_sub: Your DNA identity was received. An admin must enable you.
wait.hero_sub: DNA-Nexus is waiting for an admin to enable this new account.
wait.body_hint: This DNA-Nexus server is configured to fail-closed for new identities. Your fingerprint must be enabled by an administrator before the browser can finish sign-in.
wait.missing_k_st: Missing k (or st) in URL. Go back and start a new sign-in.
wait.expired_body: This sign-in request expired or was already consumed. Go back and start a new QR sign-in.
wait.consume_cookie_failed: Consume OK, but cookie did not stick: HTTP {status}

pqshare.open.title: Open Post-Quantum protected share
pqshare.open.lead: This shared file is protected with ML-KEM-768. The file key is unwrapped in this browser and the file is decrypted locally before download.
pqshare.open.done: Done. Integrity verified and download started.
pqshare.invite.requires_mlkem: This invite requires ML-KEM-768. Legacy non-post-quantum enrollment is not supported on this page.
pqshare.invite.alg_blurb: This browser will generate a local ML-KEM-768 keypair. The file key is wrapped for this browser using post-quantum key encapsulation, and the shared file is decrypted locally in your browser.
pqshare.invite.missing_recipient_device: Enrollment succeeded but recipient_device_id is missing
pqshare.invite.missing_share_url: Enrollment succeeded but share URL is missing
pqshare.invite.title: Open post-quantum protected share
pqshare.invite.lead: This page enrolls this browser with ML-KEM-768 and then opens the shared file locally.
pqshare.invite.good: This invite is one-time and stays valid until enrollment succeeds or the invite expires.
pqshare.unlock.purpose_enroll: This browser is about to create a local device key for post-quantum share enrollment.
pqshare.unlock.purpose_open: This browser needs to unlock its local device key before it can open this protected share.
pqshare.unlock.warning: This passphrase protects your local {alg} device key stored in this browser. It is never sent to the server and cannot be recovered. If you forget it, this browser will no longer be able to open shares encrypted to this device key. You can reset and re-enroll this browser for future shares, but older shares tied to the old key will remain inaccessible here.
pqshare.unlock.ack: I understand this passphrase cannot be recovered.
pqshare.unlock.confirm_warning: Please confirm that you understand the recovery warning.
pqshare.unlock.reset_confirm: Reset this browser's local device key?\n\nThis will remove the existing local key for this browser. Older shares encrypted to the old key will no longer open here.
pqshare.error.open_init_non_json: Open init API returned non-JSON response ({status})
raidmgr.title: Storage Manager
raidmgr.subtitle: Manage storage pools
raidmgr.help.title: Storage Manager Help
raidmgr.help.subtitle: Manage storage pools, slots, drives, and RAID mode in PQ-NAS.
raidmgr.help.diagram_alt: Diagram showing Single mode and RAID1 mirroring
raidmgr.help.action_create_pool: Create new pool creates a new editable PQ-NAS pool.
raidmgr.help.action_edit_slots: Edit slots changes the saved slot layout and pool mode metadata.
raidmgr.help.action_add_slot: Add slot adds one more empty visible slot.
raidmgr.help.action_remove_slot: Remove empty slot removes one unused empty slot.
raidmgr.help.action_apply_layout: Apply layout applies one pending membership change from the saved slot layout to the real Btrfs pool.
raidmgr.help.action_remove_drive: Remove drive removes a current member drive from the pool.
raidmgr.help.action_rename: Rename changes the display name of the pool.
raidmgr.help.action_convert: Convert RAID converts between single and raid1.
raidmgr.help.action_destroy: Destroy removes the pool from PQ-NAS and can optionally wipe member drives.
raidmgr.help.single_body: Single: Capacity-focused mode with no redundancy.
raidmgr.help.raid1_body: RAID1: Mirror-focused mode with redundancy across at least two devices.
raidmgr.help.convert_warning: Converting RAID mode may run a Btrfs balance and can take a long time depending on pool size and device speed.
raidmgr.help.warn_long: Long-running operations: Add, remove, and convert operations may take a long time.
raidmgr.help.warn_preview: Preview first: When an action uses Preview and Apply, always preview before applying.
raidmgr.help.warn_wipe: Destructive wipe: Force wipe or destroy with wipe enabled can permanently erase data.
raidmgr.help.warn_old_btrfs: Be careful with old Btrfs signatures: A disk that still contains old Btrfs metadata may look like a pool member even when it is no longer active.
raidmgr.help.workflow_1: Create a pool with the desired initial mode.
raidmgr.help.workflow_3: Assign drives or add/remove them through the pool actions.
raidmgr.help.workflow_4: Use Apply layout when saved slots and live membership differ.
raidmgr.help.workflow_5: Use Convert RAID only when the current device count supports the target mode.
raidmgr.note.layout_differs: Saved slot layout differs from current pool membership. Use Apply layout to make Btrfs match the saved layout.
raidmgr.note.layout_matches: Saved slot layout matches the current pool membership.
raidmgr.note.system_volume: This is a detected Btrfs system volume. It is shown for visibility, not managed as a normal pool.
raidmgr.no_managed_body1: Storage Manager has nothing to manage on this server right now.
raidmgr.no_managed_body2: This usually means DNA-Nexus server is currently using a single storage volume, or Btrfs pool mounts have not been created / mounted yet.
raidmgr.no_managed_body3_suffix: and that is fine. What matters here is whether DNA-Nexus server has managed Btrfs pools available.
raidmgr.no_managed_body4: When Btrfs pools exist, they will appear here automatically.
raidmgr.tiering.title: Tiering
raidmgr.tiering.note: Shows tiering-managed files only. Workspace files are currently excluded.
raidmgr.convert.title: Convert RAID
raidmgr.convert.same_mode: Target mode is the same as current mode.
raidmgr.convert.raid1_requires_2: RAID1 requires at least 2 current member devices.
raidmgr.convert.to_raid1_hint: Converts current data/metadata profiles to RAID1.
raidmgr.convert.to_single_hint: Converts current data/metadata/system profiles to SINGLE.
raidmgr.convert.cannot_raid1_fewer_2: Cannot convert to RAID1 because the pool currently has fewer than 2 devices.
raidmgr.convert.will_balance_raid1: This will run a Btrfs balance to convert data/metadata profiles to RAID1.
raidmgr.convert.will_balance_single: This will run a Btrfs balance to convert data/metadata/system profiles to SINGLE.
raidmgr.convert.toast_already_current: Target mode is already current mode.
raidmgr.convert.toast_raid1_requires_2: RAID1 requires at least 2 current member devices.
raidmgr.create.title: Create storage pool
raidmgr.create.slot_count_help: How many drive slots this pool should show
raidmgr.create.raid1_hint: RAID1 needs at least 2 assigned slots.
raidmgr.create.single_hint: Single uses one or more drives without redundancy.
raidmgr.create.assigned_devices: Assigned devices: {count}. Empty slots are allowed and become visible placeholders in Pool Manager.
raidmgr.create.bad_mode: mode must be single or raid1
raidmgr.create.assign_one_disk: Assign at least one disk to a slot.
raidmgr.create.raid1_requires_2: raid1 requires at least 2 assigned slots.
raidmgr.create.duplicate_disk: The same disk cannot be selected in multiple slots.
raidmgr.edit_slots.title: Edit pool slots
raidmgr.edit_slots.assigned_devices: Assigned devices: {count}. Empty slots stay as placeholders.
raidmgr.remove.cannot_only_devices: Cannot remove a drive: pool has only {count} device(s).
raidmgr.remove.force_label: Force (allow removing the currently-used pool drive)
raidmgr.remove.keep_off: Keep OFF unless you know exactly why you need it.
raidmgr.remove.preview_first_to_apply: Click Preview first to enable Apply.

admin.users.migrate_confirm_subtitle: This will create an async storage migration job.
admin.users.migrate_confirm_note: The worker will copy data, verify it, then switch the user's storage mapping. The old copy is kept until cleanup.
admin.users.cleanup_confirm_subtitle: This removes the old passive copy after a successful storage migration.
admin.users.cleanup_confirm_note: This deletes the old user subtree from the old pool. The currently active pool is protected.
raidmgr.destroy.title: Destroy storage pool
raidmgr.destroy.warning_body: This will unmount the pool and remove it from PQ-NAS pools config. Optionally it can wipe member disks (VERY destructive).
raidmgr.destroy.wipe_help: When ON, PQ-NAS will wipefs/sgdisk each member device after unmount.
admin.users.cleanup_prompt_subtitle: User is currently active on {pool}. Choose the old pool copy to remove.
admin.users.cleanup_prompt_note: Enter the inactive old pool id to delete. The active pool is protected.
admin.users.already_allocated_subtitle: Change this user's storage pool or quota anyway?
admin.users.already_allocated_note: This updates an existing storage allocation instead of creating a new one.
admin.users.avatar_remove_confirm_subtit: The profile will return to the default generated avatar.
admin.users.avatar_remove_confirm_note: This removes the uploaded avatar image reference from this user profile.

snapshotmgr.title: Snapshot Manager
snapshotmgr.snapshot_now.title: Snapshot now
snapshotmgr.snapshot_now.name_help: Optional name. Leave empty for automatic snapshot ID.
snapshotmgr.status.snapshots_disabled_fo: Snapshots are disabled for {volume} in Admin Settings.
snapshotmgr.tooltip.no_sudo: No sudo privileges to verify btrfs subvolume (sudo -n failed)
snapshotmgr.tooltip.junk: This folder is not a btrfs snapshot subvolume (junk under snap_root)
snapshotmgr.no_snapshots_hint: Check snap_root path and snapshot runner output.
snapshotmgr.status.volume_disabled_loadi: Volume {volume} is disabled for new snapshots. Loading existing snapshots…
snapshotmgr.status.selected_no_sudo: Selected: {id} (cannot verify btrfs subvolume; sudo rule missing)
snapshotmgr.status.snapshots_loaded_disa: Snapshots loaded for {volume}. Snapshots are disabled for this volume, so manual create is unavailable.
snapshotmgr.banner.snapshots_disabled_ht: ⚠ Snapshots are disabled for volume {volume} in Admin Settings. Existing snapshots can still be viewed, but Snapshot now is unavailable.
snapshotmgr.banner.sudo_required_html: ⚠ Snapshot verification needs sudo privileges on this host. {count} item(s) could not be verified. {link}
snapshotmgr.banner.junk_items: Note: {count} item(s) under snap_root are not btrfs snapshot subvolumes (junk folders).
snapshotmgr.restore.title: Restore
snapshotmgr.restore.waiting_online: Waiting for PQ-NAS to come back online (temporary 502 is expected).
snapshotmgr.restore.disabled_not_verifie: Restore disabled: snapshot not verified as a btrfs subvolume (or sudo missing).
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm1.title: Restore snapshot?
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm1.subtitle: This will replace the live volume content.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm1.warning: This will REPLACE the live volume content. Downtime required.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm1.note: A restore job will stop and restart PQ-NAS while the volume is swapped.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm2.title: Confirm restore
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm2.subtitle: Typed confirmation is required before restore can continue.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm2.warning: You are restoring snapshot {id} into live volume {volume}.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm3.title: Proceed with restore plan?
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm3.subtitle: Server has prepared the restore plan.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm3.warning: Proceeding starts the restore job now.
snapshotmgr.restore.confirm3.note: PQ-NAS may be temporarily unreachable during restore.
snapshotmgr.restore.no_job_id: Restore started but server did not return job_id
snapshotmgr.restore.timeout: Restore timed out waiting for result (job {job_id})
snapshotmgr.details.sudo_required_body_h: Snapshot Manager calls sudo -n btrfs subvolume show .... If sudo prompts for a password, probing becomes no-privs and restore stays disabled.
